title: Stacking contexts and z-order

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Stacking contexts and z-order; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Stacking contexts and z-order
sources.claim: Painting order and stacking contexts determine overlap; z-index compares boxes within the same relevant stacking context rather than globally.

sources.locator: MDN CSS Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Stacking contexts and z-order
sources.claim: Painting order and stacking contexts determine overlap; z-index compares boxes within the same relevant stacking context rather than globally.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Painting order and stacking contexts determine overlap; z-index compares boxes within the same relevant stacking context rather than globally.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A tooltip remains under a modal because its ancestor stacking context is below the modal, despite a large child z-index. Your goal is to diagnose and repair an overlay ordering defect using stacking-context evidence. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Box model, sizing and overflow; html semantics landmarks; Relative, absolute, fixed and sticky positioning. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Painting order and stacking contexts determine overlap; z-index compares boxes within the same relevant stacking context rather than globally. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Opacity, transforms, positioned elements, isolation, and top-layer features can create contexts that trap high z-index descendants. This draft therefore rejects: The largest z-index anywhere in the document always appears on top.

includes: Explain paint order and stacking-context boundaries instead of escalating z-index values blindly.
Diagnose and repair an overlay ordering defect using stacking-context evidence.
excludes: Opacity, transforms, positioned elements, isolation, and top-layer features can create contexts that trap high z-index descendants.

outcomes: Diagnose and repair an overlay ordering defect using stacking-context evidence.

examples.title: Stacking contexts and z-order: concrete case
examples.situation: A tooltip remains under a modal because its ancestor stacking context is below the modal, despite a large child z-index.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A tooltip remains under a modal because its ancestor stacking context is below the modal, despite a large child z-index.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Inspect stacking-context ancestry; z-index values only compete within the applicable context.
examples.result: The result follows because Inspect stacking-context ancestry; z-index values only compete within the applicable context.

examples.title: Stacking contexts and z-order: boundary case
examples.situation: Opacity, transforms, positioned elements, isolation, and top-layer features can create contexts that trap high z-index descendants.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Opacity, transforms, positioned elements, isolation, and top-layer features can create contexts that trap high z-index descendants.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: The largest z-index anywhere in the document always appears on top.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Inspect stacking-context ancestry; z-index values only compete within the applicable context.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Stacking contexts and z-order, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Stacking contexts and z-order before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
